summary: Drew DeVault's February 2022 status update covers progress across his major projects. The godocs.io fork reached version 1.0, providing continuity for godoc.org while simplifying the codebase. His programming language saw new modules including Redis protocol support, OpenGL bindings, linear algebra libraries, cryptography additions, and MIME support. SourceHut hired its third full-time engineer and landed GraphQL-native webhooks for git.sr.ht.
key_points: godocs.io 1.0 released, successfully forking and improving upon the original gddo codebase
The Hare programming language gained Redis protocol support, OpenGL bindings, linear algebra libraries, and cryptography modules including AES-XTS
SourceHut hired Conrad Hoffmann as their third full-time engineer, continuing to grow the team
A call for contributors to help build terminal support libraries equivalent to ncurses/libtickit and libvterm
money_quotes: I'm very pleased that, following our fork, we were not only able to provide continuity for godoc.org, but also to simplify, refactor, and improve the underlying software considerably.
This is fairly straightforward, though laborsome: it involves developing libraries in our language which provide the equivalents of something like ncurses.
Another month of free software development goes by with lots of progress in all respects.
key_insight: Steady, incremental progress across multiple free software projects — a language ecosystem, a documentation platform, and a development forge — demonstrates the compounding returns of sustained open-source investment.
